prioritized_contacts.name: Dan Kučera
prioritized_contacts.title: Chief Operating Officer
prioritized_contacts.relevance: Economic Buyer / Budget Holder. New market entry opportunity. Active hiring — growth signal.
pain_points: Overspending on point solutions that don't integrate
Hard to quantify ROI of BIM investment
Talent retention -- engineers want modern tools
Client demands for digital deliverables increasing
ROI justification for design software investments
Digital transformation roadmap and execution
prioritized_contacts.talk_track: Your team is on your tools today. Many firms your size are consolidating to Collections to save 30-40%% on per-seat licensing while giving teams access to the full suite.
signal_hooks: Growing headcount means growing license needs --- Flex tokens give you elastic capacity.

prioritized_contacts.relevance: Technical Champion / Daily User Advocate. New market entry opportunity. Active hiring — growth signal.
pain_points: Managing multiple disconnected tools across disciplines

expansion_plays.name: BIM Authoring & Quality (Revit + AEC Collection)
expansion_plays.what_to_sell: AEC Collection with Revit, Civil 3D, Navisworks for full multi-discipline BIM workflow.

discovery_q: Which disciplines do you coordinate in-house vs. externally?

expansion_plays.name: Competitive Displacement (SolidWorks / Creo / Solid Edge)
expansion_plays.what_to_sell: Migration to Inventor or Fusion 360 from competitive CAD, leveraging interoperability and data import tools.
expansion_plays.why_now: Company is actively using competitor CAD tools — renewal cycles and dissatisfaction create natural switching windows.
discovery_q: When is your next CAD renewal cycle?
What are the biggest frustrations with your current CAD platform?
How much time do you spend on file conversions for suppliers or customers?

expansion_plays.name: Expand Existing Autodesk Footprint
expansion_plays.what_to_sell: Upgrade existing Autodesk tools to Collection or add complementary products (e.g., Vault to existing Inventor, Navisworks to existing Revit).
expansion_plays.why_now: Company already uses Autodesk products — expansion is lower-friction than net-new and addresses gaps in their current workflow.
discovery_q: Which Autodesk tools are you using today and on what versions?
Where do you feel the biggest gaps in your current workflow?
Are you on individual licenses or a Collection?
